title: Pre-registered multi-market data protocol (specification only)

prior_chain.D9: DATA_LIMITED_INCONCLUSIVE (only NQ had clean local data)

purpose: Define fair data requirements and PASS/FAIL gates for a FUTURE S26 multi-market robustness test (S26-D11) BEFORE any new MNQ/ES/MES data is run or viewed. Mirrors the D4-before-D5 pre-registration discipline. No data fetched, decoded, or inspected in this step.
frozen_engine_reference: engine/trend_sr_ema_rsi_daily.py (UNCHANGED; the D11 run must use this exact frozen logic)

required_markets.notes: NQ is the reference only (already evaluated D3-D9, not independent). MNQ is the micro of NQ and is NOT an independent market (same underlying index); it tests instrument/contract-size robustness, not market robustness. ES and MES track the S&P 500 and are the genuinely independent underlying. MES is the micro of ES (same underlying as ES, not independent of ES).

independence_map.NQ_MNQ: same underlying (Nasdaq-100). MNQ confirms NQ only as a micro-contract replica, NOT as an independent market.
independence_map.ES_MES: same underlying (S&P 500). MES confirms ES only as a micro-contract replica, NOT as an independent market.
independence_map.NQ_ES: different underlyings (Nasdaq-100 vs S&P 500) — these count as TWO independent markets.
independence_map.independent_market_count_rule: Count independent markets by distinct underlying index, not by ticker. {NQ or MNQ} = 1 independent market (Nasdaq). {ES or MES} = 1 independent market (S&P). Maximum independent markets reachable from this symbol set beyond NQ = 1 (the S&P, via ES and/or MES).

file_naming_convention.c0_meaning: continuous front-month, roll-adjusted contract series (same convention as the committed NQ files).
file_naming_convention.rule: D11 must discover files ONLY by this exact pattern. A market with zero matching files for all required years is DATA_FAIL (as in D9). No other directory is read; data_offline/ is the only data source by design.

column_types.ts_event: ISO-8601 date or datetime parseable by datetime.fromisoformat (e.g. 2019-01-02 or 2019-01-02T00:00:00)

ohlcv_schema.rule: Any file missing a required column, or with an unparseable ts_event, or with a non-numeric OHLC value, is REJECTED (its market cannot use it).

timestamp_date_convention.granularity: daily (1d) bars only — one row per trading day
timestamp_date_convention.sort: stream sorted ascending by ts_event before simulate (loader already does this)
timestamp_date_convention.timezone: naive/exchange-local as in the existing NQ files; must be consistent within a market

IS.rule: use where available; a market may have fewer IS years

windows.separation: IS and OOS streamed SEPARATELY per market; NO pooling across windows; standalone OOS warmup convention preserved (same as D5/D9). Hard assertion in the D11 runner must refuse IS/OOS file overlap.

minimum_coverage_requirements.rationale: Mirrors the D4/D5 protocol (OOS n>=20, ideal >=30). A market/window below these floors is reported but flagged LOW_SAMPLE and cannot by itself upgrade the verdict.
minimum_coverage_requirements.below_floor_handling: A window below its bar floor or below 20 trades is NOT a fair confirmation; it is reported with metrics but marked LOW_SAMPLE/INSUFFICIENT and excluded from ROBUST_SUPPORT counting.

invalid_ohlc_rows.definition: high<low, or any of OHLC <=0, or high<max(open,close), or low>min(open,close)

calendar_gaps.tolerance: weekends/holidays expected; a gap >10 consecutive calendar days WITHIN a declared coverage year is flagged GAP_WARNING; >25% of a year's expected ~252 sessions missing => that year REJECTED for that market
calendar_gaps.note: gaps are descriptive QA; they do not get patched or interpolated.

duplicate_gap_tolerance.no_interpolation_rule: Missing bars are never filled, interpolated, or synthesized. A gap is reported, not repaired.

partial_window_coverage.context: MNQ and MES micro contracts launched 2019 (CME micro e-mini equity index launch, May 2019). They therefore CANNOT have a full 2013-2022 IS window. ES and NQ (and their continuous c0 series) extend back to/through 2013.
partial_window_coverage.rule: When a market's data begins after the IS window start, D11 must DOCUMENT the actual available start date (first ts_event present) and classify the coverage as PARTIAL_IS, not silently treat the shortened window as a full IS.

coverage_classes.FULL: data spans the full required window (IS 2013-2022 and/or OOS 2023-2025) above the bar floors
coverage_classes.PARTIAL_IS: IS present but starts after 2013-01-01 (e.g. MNQ/MES from ~2019); report actual start date; usable only if it still meets the IS bar floor (>=500 bars) and >=20 trades, else INSUFFICIENT/LOW_SAMPLE
coverage_classes.OOS_ONLY: no usable IS, only 2023-2025 present; can contribute OOS evidence but cannot be a full IS+OOS confirmation
coverage_classes.INSUFFICIENT: below bar floor or below trade-count floor for the window
coverage_classes.DATA_FAIL: no matching clean files at all

partial_window_coverage.effect_on_confirmation: A PARTIAL_IS market may still confirm IF its shortened IS and its OOS each meet floors and pass the per-confirming-market thresholds; but the report must flag the shortened IS so the comparison is honest. A micro's partial window does not relax the no-proxy rule.
partial_window_coverage.no_backfill: A short window is never extended by borrowing the big-contract's earlier data (that would violate the no-proxy rule).

data_rejection_rules: Missing required schema column -> REJECT file.
Unparseable ts_event or non-numeric OHLC -> REJECT file.
Year with >25% sessions missing -> REJECT that year for that market.
Window below bar floor (IS<500 / OOS<200) -> window marked INSUFFICIENT (not counted for ROBUST_SUPPORT).
Window with <20 trades -> marked LOW_SAMPLE (not counted as a fair confirmation).
Zero matching files for a market across all required years -> market = DATA_FAIL.
Any rejection is logged in the D11 report with the exact reason; rejected data is NOT silently dropped.

no_proxy_rule.statement: MNQ may NOT be treated as NQ, and MES may NOT be treated as ES, for the purpose of INDEPENDENT confirmation. A micro contract is a same-underlying replica, not a new market.
no_proxy_rule.allowed_use: A micro (MNQ/MES) MAY be run and reported as a same-underlying robustness/replication check, but it must be EXPLICITLY marked role='proxy_micro' and EXCLUDED from the independent-market count.
no_proxy_rule.forbidden: No substituting one symbol's data for another. No relabeling. No averaging a micro into its big-contract result. No counting NQ+MNQ as two independent markets, nor ES+MES as two.
no_proxy_rule.independent_count_definition: Independent markets = distinct underlying indices with clean, non-rejected, non-LOW_SAMPLE data. From this symbol set, NQ-side and ES-side are the only two possible independent underlyings.

symbol_to_loader_convention.rule: The D11 runner maps each symbol to its glob and role from THIS table only. No new loader code beyond a thin discovery/streaming adapter that reuses load_daily_bars + simulate + summarize unchanged. If a symbol needs a different timestamp column, that is a data-prep defect to REPORT first, not to silently adapt.

future_d11_gates.evaluation_precedence: WEAK_OR_NO_SUPPORT -> DATA_LIMITED_INCONCLUSIVE -> NQ_ONLY_SUPPORT -> PARTIAL_SUPPORT -> ROBUST_SUPPORT (first matching, evaluated from strongest downgrade upward; final label is the highest tier whose conditions are fully met and no stronger downgrade fires).

conditions: >=2 INDEPENDENT markets beyond NQ are runnable (clean, non-rejected, not LOW_SAMPLE)

NOTE: from this symbol set only 1 independent market (S&P via ES) is reachable, so ROBUST_SUPPORT is UNREACHABLE unless a 3rd-underlying market (e.g. a non-equity-index) is added to the protocol later
every confirming independent market: IS total R > 0 AND OOS total R > 0 (or OOS legitimately absent with documented reason)
every confirming market: positive without top-3 winners (else flagged winner-dependent and downgraded)
no single year > 50% of that market's window net (else downgrade)
each window meets coverage + trade-count floors
exactly 1 independent market beyond NQ is runnable AND confirms (IS>0 and OOS>0, positive without top-3, no single-year dominance, meets floors)
this is the realistic best-case from the current symbol set (ES-side confirming NQ-side)
MNQ/MES proxy_micro results may be reported as corroboration but do NOT upgrade beyond PARTIAL_SUPPORT
>=1 independent market beyond NQ was RUNNABLE (clean data, met floors) but FAILED to confirm (IS<=0 or OOS<=0, or winner-dependent, or single-year-dominated)
i.e. the edge was tested off-NQ and did not transfer
fewer than 1 independent market beyond NQ is runnable (all DATA_FAIL / REJECTED / INSUFFICIENT / LOW_SAMPLE)
i.e. the transfer question cannot be fairly answered for lack of clean data
this is the D9 outcome and remains the default until clean independent data exists
the frozen logic is non-positive across the available markets (including NQ degrading), OR
every runnable independent market fails AND results depend on a single lucky cluster

minimum_evidence_thresholds.note_on_reachability: Only 1 independent market (S&P, via ES) is reachable from {NQ,MNQ,ES,MES}. Therefore the maximum attainable verdict from this symbol set is PARTIAL_SUPPORT. Reaching ROBUST_SUPPORT requires expanding the protocol to a 3rd distinct underlying in a future amendment.

per_confirming_market.OOS_total_r: > 0 (or OOS absent with documented reason; absent OOS cannot count as confirmation)
per_confirming_market.positive_without_top3: required; else flagged winner-dependent and the market is downgraded
per_confirming_market.single_year_dominance: no single year > 50% of window net; else classification downgraded one tier

minimum_evidence_thresholds.reporting_floor: drawdown (max_drawdown_r) and trade_count MUST be reported for every market/window, including rejected/insufficient ones (with the reason).

hard_constraints_for_d11: Use the frozen engine UNCHANGED; no parameter, rule, or variant changes.
Run each market/window exactly once; no reruns, no peeking-then-adjusting.
Pre-register (this D10) is binding; D11 may not alter these gates after seeing data.
Offline-only: no fetch, no decode/transcode in the run step (data must already be clean CSV in data_offline/).
Donchian/S23/S24 untouched; templates/base.html untouched; no unrelated repo files touched.
verdict_note: This step only registers the protocol and future gates. No market was run, no data fetched/decoded/inspected, no parameters touched. S26 remains not deployable.
notes_of_record: Specification only — no backtest, no data run, no engine/test change.
Pre-registered BEFORE any MNQ/ES/MES data is run or viewed (D4-before-D5 discipline).
Key honest constraint: the {NQ,MNQ,ES,MES} set yields at most ONE independent underlying beyond NQ (the S&P via ES/MES), so PARTIAL_SUPPORT is the ceiling unless a 3rd underlying is added later.
No temp runner or generated artifact is produced by this step.
